issues.summary: Custom App Group not visible in "Unexposed Target Audience duplication within the Samsung Universe" section
issues.description:
When a user creates a Custom App Group, it appears correctly in the **"Unexposed Audience Profile → Where can the advertiser reach Unexposed Audience"** chart (Streaming App Type Reach), but is **not available** in the **"Unexposed Target Audience duplication within the Samsung Universe"** Venn diagram section.

This is inconsistent — if a custom app group is defined, it should be selectable/visible in both audience analysis sections of the report.  
  

![](blob:https://media.staging.atl-paas.net/?type=file&localId=6b57ed817d00&id=a53d343d-c870-4915-a34e-6e8d07281ff0&&collection=&height=1051&occurrenceKey=null&width=2472&__contextId=null&__displayType=null&__external=false&__fileMimeType=null&__fileName=null&__fileSize=null&__mediaTraceId=null&url=null)
### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a Custom App Group in the DSP
2. Run an Optimal Reach Incremental-to-AVOD report
3. Observe the "Where can the advertiser reach Unexposed Audience" chart — custom app group **is visible** (e.g., `ELMEX_APP_...` in the Displayed Channels filter)
4. Scroll to "Unexposed Target Audience duplication within the Samsung Universe" Venn diagram — custom app group **is NOT listed** in the Displayed Channels filter

### Expected Behavior

Custom App Group should appear as a selectable channel in the "Unexposed Target Audience duplication within the Samsung Universe" section, consistent with its presence in the Streaming App Type Reach chart above.

### Actual Behavior

Custom App Group only appears in the Streaming App Type Reach chart. The Venn diagram section only shows default channels (LinearTv, Commercial BVOD, IPTV, User Generated C., SVOD, AVOD).

### Example

`https://dsp.samsungads.com/b/297/insights/optimal-reach/incremental-to-avod/report/1416801`

### Impact

Advertisers and CSMs cannot see audience duplication data for their custom app groups, limiting the value of the custom grouping feature for cross-channel planning.

issues.summary: [BE] Introduce prompt versioning table in GenAI package
issues.description:
## Summary

Create a Postgres table to store GenAI prompt versions with their content and metadata, and wire it into the genai service layer — enabling version tracking and audit trail.

## Context

Currently prompts live as embedded Go files (`go:embed system.md` / `prompt.tmpl`) with a hardcoded version constant (`PromptVersion = "v1.0.0"`). The `genai_message` table already records which `prompt_version` produced each summary, but there's no DB-level registry of the versions themselves — their content, activation timestamps, or lifecycle status.

## Proposed Schema

Table: `genai_prompt_version`

* `id` (BIGSERIAL PK)
* `goal` (VARCHAR) — e.g. "optimal_reach"
* `tenant` (VARCHAR) — e.g. "pre_campaign"
* `task_type` (VARCHAR) — e.g. "summary"
* `version` (VARCHAR) — semver string, e.g. "v1.0.0"
* `system_prompt` (TEXT) — full system prompt content
* `user_prompt_template` (TEXT) — Go template content
* `status` (VARCHAR) — active / inactive / draft
* `created_at` (TIMESTAMPTZ)
* `activated_at` (TIMESTAMPTZ, nullable)
* `deactivated_at` (TIMESTAMPTZ, nullable)

Unique constraint: `(goal, tenant, task_type, version)`  
Index: `(goal, tenant, task_type, status)` for active prompt lookup

## Service Integration

* New repository interface + Postgres implementation (`pkg/genai/repository/prompt/`)
* `GetActivePrompt(ctx, goal, tenant, taskType)` — returns active version for given key
* `ContentProvider` reads prompt content from DB instead of embedded files
* `PromptVersion` returned from DB row rather than hardcoded constant
* Fallback to embedded prompts if DB row not found (graceful migration path) - TBD

## Scope

* Migration SQL file
* Seed row with current v1.0.0 optimal_reach/pre_campaign/summary prompt
* Repository interface + Postgres implementation
* Wire into `ContentProvider` and `DefaultGenAIService`
* Unit tests for repository layer

## Acceptance Criteria

* Migration creates the table with proper constraints and indexes
* Seed row inserted for current production prompt (v1.0.0)
* `make compose-dbmigrate` runs without errors
* Repository fetches active prompt by (goal, tenant, task_type)
* Service uses DB prompt when available, falls back to embedded – TBD
* Existing tests pass — no behavioral change for current flow

‌

‌

Description enhanced at 6/15/26. (Below the old version)

~~Add DB table for prompt versioning. Add SQL comment, that existing rows shouldn’t be edited.~~

~~Required columns:~~

* ~~report_goal~~
* ~~report_tenant~~
* ~~buyer_id~~
* ~~content~~
* ~~version~~
* ~~type (system/user)~~

~~Move existing prompts to DB. Add and wire repo with content provider.~~

### Summary

Add accessible labeling and keyboard interaction support to the chatbot input textarea in the Gen AI Summary component. The textarea currently lacks an accessible label, making it unannounced by screen readers.

### Context

The textarea is in chatbot-input.tsx (line 24) and has no aria-label or associated <label> element. Screen readers announce it as a generic "text area" with no indication of its purpose. This violates WCAG 2.1 Level A (SC 1.3.1 — Info and Relationships, and SC 4.1.2 — Name, Role, Value). The send icon button also lacks an accessible name.

### Fix

Add aria-label to the <Textarea> describing its purpose (e.g., aria-label={locales.ariaLabel} where the locale string is "Type a question for the AI assistant"). Add a locale key for the aria-label string in i18n so it can be translated. Add aria-label to the send icon/button (e.g., "Send message").

issues.summary: [BE] Add GET endpoint for fetching presets from AI-backend
issues.description:
### Summary

Expose a REST endpoint that returns available app group presets filtered by country. The frontend's "Presets" dropdown in the Select Applications modal will call this endpoint to populate the list of selectable presets.

### Context

* ai_reporting_service — fasthttp/router REST API
* Presets are country-specific (DR-5): the endpoint must filter by country code
* Each preset returned must include: preset ID, display name, list of apps with their IDs and weighting values
* The frontend needs enough data in a single call to auto-populate app selection and weighting when a preset is chosen (DR-3)

### Flow

1. Frontend calls `GET /presets?country={countryCode}` (or similar path)
2. Handler validates country parameter
3. Service layer queries the presets table for active presets matching the country
4. Response returns an ordered list of presets, each containing: ID, display name, and apps array (app ID + weight)

### Scope

* New handler in `pkg/handlers/` — register route via existing pattern (`RegisterXxxRoutes()`)
* Repository interface + Postgres implementation in `pkg/domain/repository/`
* Service layer in `pkg/services/` (or co-located with existing related service)
* Request validation: country code is required, must be a supported country
* Auth: existing JWT + HMAC middleware (same as other endpoints)
* Response contract: JSON array of presets with nested apps

### API Contract (proposed)

```
GET /presets?country=DE

Response 200:
{
  "presets": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "name": "German Streaming Competitors",
      "country": "DE",
      "apps": [
        {
          "appId": "abc-123",
          "weight": 100
        },
        {
          "appId": "def-456",
          "weight": 20
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Out of Scope

* CRUD endpoints for preset management (admin UI is future phase)
* Feature flag gating (presets are static config, not a toggleable feature)
* Caching (can be added later if latency is a concern)

### Dependencies

* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-1038</custom>

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Endpoint registered and accessible with valid JWT auth
- [ ] Returns presets filtered by country code, ordered by display order
- [ ] Returns empty array (not error) for countries with no presets
- [ ] Returns 400 for missing/invalid country parameter
- [ ] Repository interface with `//go:generate` mockgen directive
- [ ] Unit tests for handler (mocked service) and service (mocked repository)
- [ ] `make test` passes, coverage ≥ 80%
